blocks.content: The slow song came an hour in.

He felt it in the room before he heard it — the tempo dropping, the crowd rearranging, people moving together or stepping off the floor. Savannah pressed into Marcus. Eli drifted, smooth and inevitable, toward a girl from their English class.
Kit and Daisy stood where they were.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I said 'you want to —' and didn't finish the sentence.
She held out her hand. He took it.

They moved to the same kind of open space as before — not center, somewhere between the lights and the edge — but it was different now. Everything the same on the surface and completely different underneath. She put her hand on his shoulder. He put his at her waist. They started to move.
She leaned in — not dramatically, just a small degree of closer — and he met her there.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The one where you're thinking too loud.
He looked down at her. She was already looking up at him.
She held his gaze. She knew that wasn't true. He knew she knew.
